mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate directly raises the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hiking periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, it signals a structural break in labor demand that triggers immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Market produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0, or threshold fails to separate return distributions at p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month decline in housing starts and real sales of new homes indicates a synchronized contraction in residential demand, which transmits to broader corporate revenues and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns when both series decline simultaneously, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of high capacity utilization reflects strong manufacturing throughput and pricing power, which directly supports corporate earnings growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative returns when INDPRO rises during high capacity utilization, or regime classification fails to improve predictive accuracy over a baseline model.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index crosses above 205.0, it confirms entrenched inflationary pressures that compel aggressive monetary tightening, triggering immediate short-term equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity market rallies when CPIAUCSL exceeds 205.0, or the threshold condition fails to predict direction in >55% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in total nonfarm payrolls and the unemployment rate indicates deteriorating labor market quality and rising underemployment, which suppresses consumer spending and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns when both series move in the divergent direction, or the joint signal shows no incremental predictive power over single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth provides a direct measure of aggregate economic activity that scales corporate revenues, creating a persistent positive relationship with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative returns when GDPC1 growth is positive, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 across all 3-year rolling evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle confirms that higher borrowing costs are successfully suppressing economic activity, which accelerates equity devaluation and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns when INDPRO declines during tightening periods, or the regime filter fails to enhance signal accuracy relative to an unconditional model.
